message: beads: bd-iuvb.17 demo mouse/shortcut interactivity plan

files.patch:
@@ -1137,7 +1137,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.14","title":"Demo: Hyperlink Playground (OSC-8 + Hit Regions)","description":"Background
- OSC-8 hyperlinks and hit regions are powerful but under-explained.

Goals
- Display clickable links with hover/focus states.
- Show link registry IDs and OSC-8 sequences for education.
- Demonstrate keyboard navigation among links.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Interaction.
- Short label: "Links".
- Registry tags: "osc8", "hit", "link".

Implementation Notes
- Use LinkRegistry and HitGrid integration.
- Provide deterministic link list with sample URLs.
- Add a "raw sequence" panel for educational display.

Acceptance Criteria
- Links render with distinct styling and focus ring.
- Hover and keyboard focus highlight are visible.
- Optional panel shows link registry ID mapping and sequences.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for link registry behavior in demo state.
- Snapshot tests for normal, hover, and focus states.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with scripted focus movement and JSONL logs:
  - run_id, link_id, focus_idx, action, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:31.797391729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:05:16.757068820Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:05:16.757048522Z","close_reason":"Hyperlink Playground screen implemented with OSC-8, hover/focus, link registry panel, JSONL logging + snapshots; included in screen sweep","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.14","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:31.797391729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.15","title":"Demo: Performance Challenge Mode (Degradation Tiers)","description":"Background
- Degradation tiers and budgets are critical but not demonstrated interactively.

Goals
- Provide a "stress" mode that intentionally overwhelms rendering.
- Show real-time degradation tier transitions and recovery.
- Display budget metrics and decision evidence.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Systems.
- Short label: "Perf".
- Registry tags: "budget", "tier", "latency".

Implementation Notes
- Reuse performance HUD metrics and render budget info.
- Add deterministic stress scenarios (fixed seed, fixed workload).
- Include a "cool down" button to show recovery.

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress toggle reliably triggers degradation and recovery.
- UI clearly shows current tier and decision rationale.
- Runs without stalling input handling.
- Evidence entries are recorded for each tier change.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for stress scenario generator determinism.
- Snapshot tests for each degradation tier.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, tier_from, tier_to, frame_time_ms, decision, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:39.834624358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:03:59.234397776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T03:03:59.234379662Z","close_reason":"Completed performance challenge mode (stress harness + tier logging)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:13.446170028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.15","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:39.834624358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.16","title":"Demo: Navigation IA (Screen Registry + Palette + Category Tabs)","description":"Background
- With 30+ screens, navigation can become overwhelming without a strong IA.
- A dedicated registry and palette make the demo feel intentional and fast.

Goals
- Implement Screen Registry (id/title/short_label/category/order/palette_tags/blurb/hotkey).
- Implement category tabs + category-aware navigation.
- Implement Screen Palette (Ctrl+K) with search, filters, favorites, and ranking.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Tour/Core/Visuals/Interaction/Text/Systems displayed with color pills.
- Tab bar shows short_label + category color.
- Shift+Left/Right jumps categories; Left/Right moves within category.

Implementation Notes
- Registry is the single source of truth for screen list and ordering.
- Palette ranking: exact > prefix > word-start > substring (stable tie-breakers).
- Favorites are persisted in memory for session; optional future persistence.

Acceptance Criteria
- Registry drives tab bar, palette, and help overlay (no duplicated lists).
- Palette search is fast and deterministic (fixed ordering for equal scores).
- Category navigation works from any screen.
- All screens are registered with category, label, tags, and blurb.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for registry ordering and palette ranking.
- Snapshot tests for palette (empty, filtered, favorites) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, action, query, selected_screen, category, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacOwl","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103372323Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T07:39:45.103354269Z","close_reason":"Completed: palette logging, snapshots, ranking test, help legend, E2E step","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.16","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T03:04:32.705019824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17","title":"Demo UX: Mouse-first interactivity + shortcut expansion","description":"Background
- Demo showcase already has a strong screen catalog, Screen Registry, command palette, and a Guided Tour.
- Mouse support exists but is currently shallow: tab bar click-switching is wired via the hit grid, and a handful of screens register hit regions, but most screens do not respond to mouse in an intuitive 'click what you see' way.
- Many screens have keyboard shortcuts, but lots of interaction is either hidden or passive; the demo should feel alive and exploratory.

Goals
- Make every screen in `ftui-demo-showcase` meaningfully mouse-friendly:
  - Clickable controls/widgets respond as users expect.
  - Scroll wheel scrolls the thing under the pointer (lists, panels, editors).
  - Drag interactions work where appropriate (kanban, drag-drop, timeline scrubbing, sliders).
  - Hover affordances are visible (focus/active state, subtle highlight, status hints).
- Add more keyboard shortcuts that *do things* (not just navigation):
  - Consistent global shortcuts (help, palette, perf HUD, inspector, toggle mouse).
  - Per-screen shortcuts for toggling modes, adjusting knobs, and triggering demos.
- Upgrade the Guided Tour storyboard to include the 'best stuff' and explicitly demonstrate:
  - Inline mode value (scrollback preserved; chrome stable).
  - Determinism + checksums + evidence ledgers.
  - Hit testing + hyperlinks + inspector overlays.
  - Performance HUD + budgets + degradation behavior.

Hard Constraints / Invariants (non-negotiable)
- Preserve Inline Mode scrollback as a first-class property:
  - Default behavior must not silently steal terminal scrollback unless user opts in.
  - Mouse capture policy must be explicit and documented in the demo UI and CLI.
- Keep one-writer rule / terminal lifecycle correctness intact.
- Determinism: fixed seeds + time-freeze remain deterministic across mouse/keyboard interactions.
- Performance: hit testing and hover updates must not materially regress frame time; coalesce high-frequency mouse move events.

Scope Notes
- This epic is about demo UX and interaction plumbing. It may require small changes in `ftui-core`/`ftui-widgets` (e.g., semantic mouse events or richer hit regions) when needed, but avoid large API churn.
- Prefer building shared primitives (clickable list/table/scrollbar/etc.) once, then applying to screens.

Success Criteria
- Every screen under `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/` has:
  - At least one meaningful mouse interaction (click/scroll/drag/hover) that changes state.
  - At least 3-5 screen-specific shortcuts that are discoverable in the help overlay.
- Guided Tour v2 covers the highest-signal screens and interactions.
- Test coverage exists:
  - Unit tests for mouse dispatch + focus/hover rules.
  - Deterministic integration tests per interaction category.
  - PTY/E2E validation where feasible.

Key References (starting points)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (hit grid enablement + tab click handler)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs` (HitId ranges for chrome/panes)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (mouse capture enable/disable)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs` (semantic click/drag abstractions)
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` + `spatial_hit_index.rs` (hit grid implementation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","mouse","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-iuvb.17","title":"Demo UX: Mouse-first interactivity + shortcut expansion","description":"Background
- Demo showcase already has a strong screen catalog, Screen Registry, command palette, and a Guided Tour.
- Mouse support exists but is currently shallow: tab bar click-switching is wired via the hit grid, and a handful of screens register hit regions, but most screens do not respond to mouse in an intuitive 'click what you see' way.
- Many screens have keyboard shortcuts, but lots of interaction is either hidden or passive; the demo should feel alive and exploratory.

Goals
- Make every screen in `ftui-demo-showcase` meaningfully mouse-friendly:
  - Clickable controls/widgets respond as users expect.
  - Scroll wheel scrolls the thing under the pointer (lists, panels, editors).
  - Drag interactions work where appropriate (kanban, drag-drop, timeline scrubbing, sliders).
  - Hover affordances are visible (focus/active state, subtle highlight, status hints).
- Add more keyboard shortcuts that *do things* (not just navigation):
  - Consistent global shortcuts (help, palette, perf HUD, inspector, toggle mouse).
  - Per-screen shortcuts for toggling modes, adjusting knobs, and triggering demos.
- Upgrade the Guided Tour storyboard to include the 'best stuff' and explicitly demonstrate:
  - Inline mode value (scrollback preserved; chrome stable).
  - Determinism + checksums + evidence ledgers.
  - Hit testing + hyperlinks + inspector overlays.
  - Performance HUD + budgets + degradation behavior.

Hard Constraints / Invariants (non-negotiable)
- Preserve Inline Mode scrollback as a first-class property:
  - Default behavior must not silently steal terminal scrollback unless user opts in.
  - Mouse capture policy must be explicit and documented in the demo UI and CLI.
- Keep one-writer rule / terminal lifecycle correctness intact.
- Determinism: fixed seeds + time-freeze remain deterministic across mouse/keyboard interactions.
- Performance: hit testing and hover updates must not materially regress frame time; coalesce high-frequency mouse move events.

Scope Notes
- This epic is about demo UX and interaction plumbing. It may require small changes in `ftui-core`/`ftui-widgets` (e.g., semantic mouse events or richer hit regions) when needed, but avoid large API churn.
- Prefer building shared primitives (clickable list/table/scrollbar/etc.) once, then applying to screens.

Success Criteria
- Every screen under `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/` has:
  - At least one meaningful mouse interaction (click/scroll/drag/hover) that changes state.

- Guided Tour v2 covers the highest-signal screens and interactions.
- Test coverage exists:
  - Unit tests for mouse dispatch + focus/hover rules.
  - Deterministic integration tests per interaction category.
  - PTY/E2E validation where feasible.

Key References (starting points)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (hit grid enablement + tab click handler)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs` (HitId ranges for chrome/panes)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (mouse capture enable/disable)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs` (semantic click/drag abstractions)
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` + `spatial_hit_index.rs` (hit grid implementation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:58.741342904Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","mouse","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:43:01.674582434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":817,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution Notes / Dependency Shape
- Foundation tasks:
  - bd-iuvb.17.1 (mouse policy / inline scrollback) blocks bd-iuvb.17.2 (dispatcher) and bd-iuvb.17.6 (tour v2) and bd-iuvb.17.7 (tests).
  - bd-iuvb.17.2 (dispatcher) blocks bd-iuvb.17.4 (chrome mouse).
  - bd-iuvb.17.3 (widget hit semantics) and bd-iuvb.17.5 (shortcut standards) are shared prerequisites for essentially all screen work.
- Screen tasks are grouped under sub-epics by ScreenCategory:
  - Tour: bd-iuvb.17.8
  - Core: bd-iuvb.17.9
  - Visuals: bd-iuvb.17.10
  - Interaction: bd-iuvb.17.11
  - Text: bd-iuvb.17.12
  - Systems: bd-iuvb.17.13
- Guided Tour v2 (bd-iuvb.17.6) is explicitly blocked on the highest-signal interactive screens (dashboard, inline mode, determinism, time travel, hyperlinks, perf HUD, etc.) so we don’t ship a tour that skips the good stuff.

Suggested Parallelization
- Do bd-iuvb.17.1 and bd-iuvb.17.2 early, because they define the click/scroll/drag contract.
- bd-iuvb.17.3 (widgets) can proceed in parallel and will unblock many screens quickly.
- Once the dispatcher exists, screens can be upgraded independently by category.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","title":"Demo Input: mouse capture policy + inline scrollback preservation","description":"Background
- Mouse capture is currently available (see `ftui-core` terminal session enable/disable and the demo's `--no-mouse` CLI flag), but we must not compromise FrankenTUI's core value proposition: Inline Mode preserves native terminal scrollback.
- In many terminals, enabling mouse reporting steals the scroll wheel from the terminal's scrollback. That is acceptable in AltScreen mode, but it is a footgun in Inline mode unless it is explicit and opt-in.

Goal
- Define and implement a clear, explicit mouse capture policy for the demo showcase that:
  - Preserves native scrollback in Inline mode by default.
  - Still allows a 'full mouse' experience in AltScreen mode by default.
  - Provides an obvious, discoverable toggle and CLI/env config.

Design / Policy (proposed)
- Introduce a demo-level `MouseMode` concept (names are bikesheddable):
  - `Off`: no mouse capture; scroll wheel is terminal scrollback.
  - `On`: mouse capture enabled (click/scroll/drag/hover routed to UI).
- Defaults:
  - AltScreen: `On`.
  - Inline: `Off` (unless user explicitly opts in).
- UI affordances:
  - Status bar shows `Mouse: OFF (scrollback)` vs `Mouse: ON (UI scroll)`.
  - Help overlay documents toggle.
- Controls:
  - Keybinding: `M` toggles mouse mode.
  - CLI: keep `--no-mouse`; add `--mouse=on|off` (or env) if needed for determinism in tests.

Key Constraints
- No surprise scrollback theft in Inline mode.
- Must not violate one-writer rule or terminal teardown invariants.
- Deterministic: mode toggles are explicit inputs; no terminal-dependent heuristics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline mode demo retains native scrollback by default (wheel scrolls scrollback).
- AltScreen demo remains fully mouse interactive by default.
- Mouse toggle is visible/discoverable (status + help), and is logged in JSONL if demo logging is enabled.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for CLI/env parsing and default selection based on screen mode.
- PTY E2E smoke: Inline mode launch asserts mouse capture is disabled by default; AltScreen asserts enabled.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs` (Enable/Disable mouse capture)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` (mouse flags)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (event routing)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:39.023348895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","inline-mode","input","mouse","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.578455672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":815,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyGorge claiming this bead. Will implement mouse capture policy with MouseMode enum, screen-mode-aware defaults, M keybinding toggle, status bar indicator, and CLI flags.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Visuals screens","description":"Purpose
- Track mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for Visuals-category screens.

Included Screens
- Data Viz
- Table Theme Gallery
- Mermaid Showcase
- Mermaid Mega Showcase
- Visual Effects
- Theme Studio
- Snapshot Player (Time Travel Studio)

Visuals Interaction Themes
- Mouse for pan/zoom/inspect where meaningful (diagrams, charts).
- Wheel adjusts scale or scrolls within the appropriate panel, never 'randomly'.
- Keyboard shortcuts trigger parameter sweeps, overlays, and reset-to-deterministic-state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","visuals"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:47:54.931165079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","title":"Data Viz: click-to-toggle series + mouse inspect + shortcuts","description":"Background
- DataViz is a dense visuals screen; it should support mouse inspection and fast toggles.

Goal
- Make charts feel interactive: click legends, hover points, wheel scroll/zoom where appropriate.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover a chart point shows tooltip (in status hint or overlay).
- Click legend toggles series visibility.
- Wheel adjusts zoom scale or scrolls within the selected chart panel (consistent rule).

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `L`: toggle legend.
- `Z`: toggle zoom mode.
- `R`: reset view.

Acceptance Criteria
- Interactions are deterministic (same inputs -> same tooltip/selection).
- At least 3-5 shortcuts are available and discoverable.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/data_viz.rs`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data-viz","demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:49:40.661409127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
